title: Anemia in pregnancy
excerpt: Common symptoms, diagnosis with blood tests, iron-rich nutrition, and supplement safety — WHO guidance summarized.
content:
## Common signs

Tiredness, pale skin, breathlessness, dizziness — but labs are required to diagnose.

## Diagnosis

Hemoglobin and ferritin (or full panel) at prenatal visits. Treat cause: iron deficiency, B12, folate, or other.

## Nutritional support

Red meat, eggs, beans, greens + vitamin C; continue prescribed iron; avoid tea right after iron-rich meals.

## Notes

Too much iron is harmful — follow prescribed dose. Severe symptoms or chest pain need urgent evaluation.
